title: JAMS: Atomistic spin dynamics simulator for magnetic materials
description:
JAMS (Joe’s Awesome Magnetic Simulator) is a high-performance software package for atomistic spin dynamics simulations of magnetic materials. It implements numerical integration of the Landau–Lifshitz–Gilbert (LLG) equation with support for stochastic thermal fields, enabling the study of magnetisation dynamics, spin waves, and thermally activated processes.

Key features include:
- Atomistic Heisenberg spin models with exchange, anisotropy, dipole and external field interactions
- Multiple numerical integrators for deterministic and stochastic dynamics
- GPU acceleration for large-scale simulations
- Flexible configuration via text-based input files
- Tools for computing spectra and dynamical correlation functions

JAMS is designed for research in spintronics, ultrafast magnetism, and computational condensed matter physics.

Source code and documentation:
https://github.com/stonerlab/jams
